mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces discount rates for equity valuations, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack increases recession probabilities, causing equity risk premiums to rise and S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals broad-based domestic demand expansion, driving corporate earnings expectations higher and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with Rank IC > 0.05, or interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month acceleration in the index of industrial production reflects rapid capacity utilization recovery, which compresses input cost margins and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum predicts positive 5-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the signal shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels above 260.0 combined with a flat or rising federal funds rate indicate persistent inflationary pressures, forcing markets to price in tighter financial conditions and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint condition of high CPI and stable/rising rates fails to predict negative returns with Rank IC > 0.04, or market returns remain positive in > 65% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month gains in total nonfarm payrolls indicate robust labor demand, which supports consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive payroll growth predicts negative 60-day returns in > 50% of cases, or the signal yields Rank IC < 0.03 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 3.0 percent signals an overheating economy where demand outpaces supply, leading to margin compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: High GDP growth predicts positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances, or the negative correlation breaks down with Rank IC < 0.02 during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth surpasses 2.0 percent quarter-over-quarter, capacity constraints emerge that drive up input costs, eroding profit margins and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The threshold condition fails to predict negative 5-day returns with statistical significance, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over out-of-sample periods.
